title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Is Suicide Covered in Term Insurance?
A term insurance plan covers premature deaths. If the insured dies during the coverage tenure, the plan pays a guaranteed death benefit to the surviving family. This benefit helps the family meet its financial obligations without any hiccups. As such, term insurance cover helps compensate a bereaved family for the financial loss that they suffer. It is a protection-oriented policy which secures against unforeseen eventualities.
Besides the term insurance benefits, when it comes to the types of deaths that a term plan covers, there is no differentiation. The term plan covers both normal and accidental deaths. Even in the case of illness-related deaths, coverage is available. However, there is one exception to the coverage – suicides.
Is suicide covered under term insurance plans?
Broadly speaking, suicide is covered under term insurance plans but with certain terms and conditions attached.
Term insurance plans have a standard suicide exclusion wherein coverage is allowed only in a specified case. Have a look –
Suicides within 12 months¹:
If the insured commits suicide and dies within 12 months of buying the policy, such a death is not covered. In that case, the insurance company simply refunds the premium, and the coverage is terminated.
There is another clause associated with revivals. If a lapsed term plan is revived and then the insured dies within 12 months of such revival, the death benefit would not be paid. In that case, a higher of the following is refunded back –
80% of the premiums are paid till the death
Surrender value available under the plan as of the date of death
Remember, term insurance cover that does not have a maturity benefit has no surrender value. If the insured dies within 12 months of a revival, 80%² of the premium paid is refunded. However, term plans with the return of premium (TROP) option do have a surrender value. So, if you have bought a TROP, you will get higher of the benefits mentioned above.
Suicides after 12 months:
Once 12 months have elapsed from the date of issuance or revival of the policy, you get full coverage. In the case of suicidal deaths, the promised death benefit is paid.
Other inclusions of term insurance
Besides suicides, term insurance plans cover the following types of deaths –
Accidental deaths.
Deaths due to natural causes.
Deaths due to an illness or injury.
Term insurance exclusions:
Besides excluding suicidal deaths within the first 12 months of commencement and revival, there are other exclusions in a term plan too. In these exclusions, the claim is not paid. The exclusions include deaths that occur due to the following reasons–
Alcohol and/or substance abuse
When engaged in criminal activities or any activity that breaches the law
When participating in adventure sports or hazardous activities like bungee jumping, scuba diving, paragliding, etc.
Moreover, death claims would not be paid if you did not pay the premium on time and your policy has lapsed. In such cases, if the insured dies, the company would reject the claim since a lapsed policy means no coverage.
Similarly, if you die due to medical complications arising out of a pre-existing medical condition and you did not disclose the pre-existing condition when buying the policy, the insurer might dispute the claim. Hiding material information is a breach of good faith, a principle on which insurance plans are issued. Since you breach the principle, the insurer might reject the claim.
Conclusion
Suicides are covered under term insurance plans, provided the terms of such coverage are fulfilled. So, understand the finer details of the coverage to know whether the suicide claim would be paid or not and the exact term insurance benefits. Also, check the other exclusions of the plan so that if your claim gets rejected, you will know the reason for it.
